properties.name: Belev
notes: Located along the South Urskoy in the foothills.

wiki.title: Bolgasgrad
wiki.description: Bolgasgrad is a fairly large stanitsa, dominated by Gospodars and proud of its rota of winged lancers. Its boyars have a tradition of strong-minded independence from the central authorities, as well as a history of being replaced with appointees from Kislev.

properties.name: Enisov
notes: Isolated settlement, not a part of any Oblast

properties.name: Erengrad
notes: Independent Kislevite kingdom and the land’s major port.

wiki.title: Erengrad
wiki.description: Erengrad, originally called Norvard, is the primary trading port of the northern nation of Kislev. Erengrad is second only to the independent mercantile city-state of Marienburg as a trading port for the northern Old World, and sits at the mouth of the River Lynsk. It is the Kislevite city most likely to be visited and populated by other Old Worlders.

wiki.title: Kislev
wiki.description: Kislev, also sometimes called Kislev City, originally known as the Imperial trading town of Pelzburg, and later as the Ungol settlement of Dorogo, is the capital city of the Tzardom of Kislev.

properties.name: Pinsk
notes: Famous for its sharp and strongly “aromatic” cheeses

properties.name: Praag
notes: Independent Kislevite kingdom, bridge over North Lynsk

wiki.title: Praag
wiki.description: Praag, also known as the "Cursed City", is the largest and most heavily fortified city of northern Kislev, earning itself the title of "Bastion of the North". Though rebuilt by the stubborn Kislevites of mostly ethnic Ungol origins who call it home again and again, Praag can never shake off the legacy of its endless sieges and the destruction left behind by the constant assaults of the Norscans and other forces of Chaos.

properties.name: Preslav
notes: Largest town in the Wheatland Colonies

properties.name: Shatsk
notes: Located in the foothills of the Worlds Edge Mountains

properties.name: Zwolen
notes: Near the Imperial border where the Urskoy meets the Talabec, ferry
